name: 4x/Week Upper/Lower Split
description: Upper/Lower split with A/B variations. 4 training days + 3 Zone 2 cardio sessions. Use the same plan bulking or cutting — what built the muscle retains the muscle.
creator: @deanttraining

activity.name: Zone 2 Cardio
activity.notes: Heart rate 120-140bpm. Walk, bike, or light jog.

activity.name: Zone 2 Cardio
activity.notes: Heart rate 120-140bpm. Walk, bike, or light jog.

activity.name: Zone 2 Cardio
activity.notes: Heart rate 120-140bpm. Walk, bike, or light jog.

tips: Same leg day repeated twice per week
The plan you run while bulking should be the SAME plan you run while cutting
What built you the muscle will help you retain the muscle
Programming should remain largely unchanged when shifting from surplus to deficit
restGuidelines: ~1 RIR on all working sets. 4-5 minutes rest between sets.

overview.goal: Build and retain muscle through a balanced upper/lower split. Works equally well for bulking and cutting phases.
overview.whoIsItFor: Intermediate lifters who want a sustainable 4-day gym schedule with room for cardio on off days.
overview.whatToExpect: 4 training days per week alternating upper and lower body, with A/B variations to hit muscles from different angles. Three additional Zone 2 cardio sessions fill the rest of the week for cardiovascular health and recovery.
keyPrinciples: Same program for bulking and cutting — what built the muscle retains the muscle
All working sets at ~1 RIR (reps in reserve)
4-5 minutes rest between working sets for full recovery
A/B variations ensure balanced development across the week
Zone 2 cardio on off days builds aerobic base without interfering with recovery
overview.cycleNote: This is a weekly cycle — repeat every week. Run the same program whether bulking or cutting. Progress by adding weight or reps over time.
overview.weeklyStructure: Mon: Upper A → Tue: Lower A → Wed: Cardio → Thu: Upper B → Fri: Lower B → Sat: Cardio → Sun: Cardio
